semanticCore: Binding, covenant, firm belief, or fetter
quranicRelevance: Primordial/solemn covenant (Mithaq), binding in fetters ('wathaq'), and trusting.

keyDerivations.meaning: Covenant, treaty, pact, or solemn pledge

figurativeOrRareUsages: مِيثَاقَكُمْ: Your covenant (especially the covenant of the prophets or the children of Israel).
وَلَا يُوثِقُ وَثَاقَهُ أَحَدٌ (89:26): And none will bind as he binds.
summaryEn: The root denotes binding and covenants, undergirding the solemn covenants (Mithaq) between God and mankind and physical constraints.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الربط والعهود، مؤصلاً للميثاق الغليظ بين الله وخلقه (ميثاق) والربط المادي (وثاق).
rawText: أَخَذَ الأَمْرَ بِالأَوْثَقِ He set about the affair in the surest, or firmest, manner. (TA.) أَوْثَقَهُ 4 He made it firm, stable, fast, or strong. (Msb.) === He bound, or tied, him, or it, firmly, fast, or strongly, in a bond. (S, K, TK.) See شَدَّهُ. الوَثِيقَةُ فِى الأَمْرِ The doing the thing firmly; and taking the sure method, or way; expl. by إِحْكَامُهُ والأَخْذُ بِالثَِّقَةِ. (TA.) === وَثِيقَةٌ A bond, security, or writing of obligation for the payment of a debt or the like: pl. وَثَائِقُ. Ex., كِتْبَةُ الوَثَائِقِ (Bd, ii. 282,) The mode of writing bonds. === A pledge. === وَثِيقَةٌ i. q. دُرْجَةٌ, q. v. اِسْتَوْثَقَ مِنْهُ 10 [He secured himself against him, by a bond or the like, or absolutely:] he took, or received, a bond (وَثِيقَة) from him. (S, K.) === أُكْتُبُوهُ ٱسْتِيثَاقًا وَدَفْعًا لِلنِّزَاعِ [Write ye it, (namely, the debt,) for the creditor's self-securing, and for preventing contention]. (Jel, ii. 282.) See اِسْتَظْهَرَ. === اِسْتَوْثَقَ مِنَ البَابِ He closed the door firmly. (MA.) === اِسْتَوْثَقَ مِنْ أُمُورِهِ: see أَعْنَجَ. === اِسْتَوْثَقَ مِنَ المَالِ: see استوثج. === اِسْتَوْثَقَ بِهِ He confided in him. تَوَثَّقَتِ العُقْدَةُ 5 [The knot became firm, or fast]. (A, in art. ارب.) ثِقَةٌ Trusty; trustworthy; honest: applied as an epithet alike to a man and a woman, and to two or more men or women: pl. ثِقَاتٌ. (Msb.) === بِهِ ثِقَتِى In him is my trust, or confidence. (TA.) === لَسْتُ مِنْهُ عَلَى ثِقَةٍ I am not confident, or sure, of it. Occurring in the S, art. ضمر, &c. See an ex. voce شَهْرٌ (last sentence). === أَخُو ثِقَةٍ: see أَخٌ. === أَخَذَ بِالثِّقَةِ: see أَخَذَ, and see my explanation of إِحْتَاطَ. مَوْثُوقٌ بِهِ In whom [and in which] trust, or confidence, is placed. (Mgh.) مَوْثِقٌ and 🡻مِيثَاقٌ A compact; a contract; a covenant; an agreement; a league; a treaty; an engagement; a bond; an obligation; a promise. (S, K, &c.) مُوَثَّقَةُ الخَلْقِ A she-camel rendered firm, strong, or compact, in make. (S.) مِيثَاقٌ : see مَوْثِقٌ; and Jel, &c., in ii. 60 and 87. وَثُقَ 1 It was, or became, firm, stable, fast, or strong; (Msb;) i. q. أُحْكِمَ (S, Mgh, K) [or اِسْتَحْكَمَ, q. v.]. === وَثِقَ بِهِ He trusted, or confided, in him. (S, Mgh, Msb, K.)
